channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: China Sees a ‘Giant With a Limp’ as U.S. Drains Weapons on Iran War
item.description: America’s ability to deter China in a war over Taiwan is weakened, Chinese analysts say, giving Beijing leverage in an upcoming summit with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson and Berry Wang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Center for Strategic and International Studies
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: President Trump meeting with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, in Busan, South Korea, in October. Both sides agreed to extend a trade truce to limit tariffs.

item.title: Trump Reversed Hormuz Plan After Saudis Denied Airspace Access
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s refusal of support suggests that President Trump’s unpredictable approach to Iran has strained ties with one of his closest allies in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley, Eric Schmitt and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman of Saudi Arabia at the White House with President Trump in November.

item.title: How Energy Prices Are Driving Demand for Solar Panels and Heat Pumps
item.description: Already having faced an energy crisis five years ago, more European consumers believe they are “one Trump-ignited war away” from crushing costs.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: David Payr for The New York Times
item.media:description: A worker in Vienna installing a heat pump for the company Heizma, which said it had record sales in March and April.

item.title: Trade Court Rules Trump’s 10% Global Tariff Is Illegal
item.description: A panel of federal judges found that President Trump could not legally impose the tariff on most imports.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
item.media:description: A court found that President Trump had wrongly invoked a decades-old trade law when he applied the duties in February.

item.title: U.S. Debt Hits a Concerning Milestone, and Experts Say Trump’s Policies Could Worsen It
item.description: The debt is outgrowing the size of America’s economy. The president’s policies could accelerate the country’s fiscal headaches, experts say, unless policymakers intervene.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The reaction to the U.S. debt has appeared muted on Capitol Hill, despite years of hand-wringing among Republicans, in particular, about the need for fiscal restraint.

item.title: Why Stocks and Bonds Are Responding Differently to the Iran War
item.description: Stock investors are betting that companies will make enormous profits, despite the war. But investors in bonds, including U.S. Treasuries, have other concerns.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Sommer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People attending a pro-government demonstration in Tehran last month. The S&P 500 has hit new highs since the start of the war in Iran.

item.title: U.S. to Review Mexican Consulates After Right-Wing Claims Against Them
item.description: The State Department said it would review all Mexican consulates in the United States, following claims in conservative media that the consulates interfere in politics.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas
item.media:credit: Alisha Jucevic for The New York Times
item.media:description: The State Department is reviewing the 53 Mexican consulates in the U.S., including the consulate in Los Angeles.

item.title: Health Officials Race to Track Hantavirus Cruise Ship Outbreak but Predict ‘Limited’ Spread
item.description: A Dutch flight attendant and French national were among the latest to be tested in connection with a deadly outbreak on a cruise ship. But an analysis in South Africa showed no mutations in viral samples.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses, Aimee Ortiz and Rylee Kirk
item.media:description: An ambulance during an evacuation of suspected hantavirus patients, in Praia, Cape Verde, on Wednesday.

item.title: Should You Worry About Hantavirus?
item.description: Here’s what to know about the virus, how it spreads and the risk to the general public.
item.dc:creator: Nina Agrawal and Dani Blum

item.title: Hantavirus Response Shows How Trump Cuts Have Compromised U.S. Preparedness
item.description: The Trump administration has slashed funding for infectious disease research and has far fewer employees, including disease detectives, to respond to outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: An ambulance crew awaiting an evacuated passenger with a suspected hantavirus infection in Amsterdam on Wednesday. The U.S. government has released very little information about the outbreak on a cruise ship, which has affected several Americans.

item.title: Is the Blake Lively v. Justin Baldoni Legal Fight Over? Not So Fast.
item.description: Lawyers for Ms. Lively and Mr. Baldoni have said the two sides settled much of the contentious dispute. But one major disagreement remains.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:description: Despite the joint statement they put out, Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni have not settled a major issue that divides them: Is she due damages in a related legal matter?

item.title: Many Russians in No Mood for Celebration on Kremlin’s Biggest Day of the Year
item.description: As the country heads into Victory Day, the economy is stalling, internet restrictions are growing and the Kremlin is fearing Ukrainian attacks.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Nanna Heitmann
item.category: Communist Party of the Russian Federation
item.media:description: Red Square in Moscow. The Victory Day procession, one of the year’s biggest events, is staged in the heart of Kremlin power.

item.title: Fetterman Says He Has ‘No Plans’ to Leave the Democratic Party
item.description: In an opinion piece in The Washington Post, the Pennsylvania senator wrote that while he was “at odds” with the party’s approach to some issues, his values had not changed.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:description: Senator John Fetterman, Democrat of Pennsylvania, wrote in an opinion piece in The Washington Post this week that the Democratic Party had veered off course in its stance on immigration and the war in Iran.

item.title: Tennessee Approves New Map Aimed at Flipping the Last Democratic Seat
item.description: After a Supreme Court ruling that weakened the Voting Rights Act of 1965, Republicans carved up a majority-Black Memphis seat as the national redistricting wars continue.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Brad J. Vest for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Steve Cohen, left, Tennessee State Senator Raumesh Akbari, center, and Tennessee State Senator London Lamar, right, discuss the new House map unveiled by Tennessee Republicans on Wednesday.

item.title: A Project in Europe Tested Whether Students Could Live Without Smartphones
item.description: For three weeks, thousands of children in Europe went without social media or their smartphones, or both. This is the diary of their digital detox.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova
item.media:description: A teenager using a smartphone in Vienna. Some participants in a phone ban experiment felt more connected to their families and hobbies, while others struggled without the instant digital stimulus.

item.title: Vote Brings ‘Freeze the Rent’ Closer to Reality in New York
item.description: The panel that regulates rents for nearly one million apartments cast its first vote since Mayor Zohran Mamdani took office, approving ranges that included no increases.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri
item.media:description: As New York City’s Rent Guidelines Board provisionally voted on a rent freeze, a member of the audience made their views clear.

item.title: Canvas Online Learning Platform Disabled After Breach by Hackers
item.description: Most users regained access to the platform hours after a hacking group said it had attacked Canvas’s parent company and breached 275 million people’s data.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Harvard is one of the schools that experienced Canvas outages on Thursday.

item.title: Philip Caputo, Who Wrote Blistering Vietnam War Memoir, Dies at 84
item.description: “A Rumor of War,” about his service as a Marine Corps infantry officer and published in 1977, relentlessly detailed “the things men do in war and the things war does to them.”
item.dc:creator: Joseph Berger
item.media:credit: Frank Lennon/Toronto Star, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Philip Caputo in Canada, in 1987.

item.title: New NTSB Report Into Deadly China Eastern Crash Suggests Struggle in Cockpit
item.description: A report by the U.S. National Transportation Safety Board offers new details about the China Eastern Airlines crash in 2022, which killed all 132 people on board.
item.dc:creator: James Glanz
item.media:description: Emergency workers searching for the black box at the crash site of the China Eastern flight in Teng County, China in 2022.

item.title: Here’s How Freaked Out You Should Be About the Hantavirus Cruise Ship
item.description: An expert weighs in.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Rivers

item.title: Is Trump About to Invite In the Biggest Predator in the World?
item.description: Chinese automakers do not belong on American soil.
item.dc:creator: Oren Cass

item.title: Late Night Recaps Rubio’s Peacemaking Visit to Pope Leo
item.description: Jimmy Fallon joked that President Trump and Pope Leo are putting Marco Rubio, who is Catholic, “in the middle and seeing which parent he goes to.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Rubio Meets Pope Leo Amid Growing Tensions Between Trump and Vatican
item.description: The meeting at the Vatican followed President Trump’s condemnation of Pope Leo XIV for opposing the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV meeting with Secretary of State Marco Rubio at the Vatican in a photograph released Thursday by Vatican Media.

item.title: Jason Meade Found Guilty in Fatal Shooting of Casey Goodson Jr. in Ohio
item.description: Jason Meade had been searching for a fugitive when he shot Casey Goodson Jr. in 2020. It is rare for a law enforcement official to be convicted in a killing that occurs on the job.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.media:description: Jason Meade in court in Columbus, Ohio, on Wednesday.

item.title: German Tourist Wins Lawsuit After Not Getting a Lounge Chair by the Pool
item.description: A tourist won a refund after a court ruled that the hotel’s staff had failed to stop guests from reserving sun beds using towels.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:credit: Robert Rausch for The New York Times
item.media:description: A German court ruled in April that a tour operator that tolerates placing dibs on hotel sun beds is liable for a travel defect and must refund part of the holiday price to any guest left out.

item.title: After Stumbles, Technology Meant for Self-Driving Cars Finds a Second Act
item.description: After a crush of promises in 2016 that autonomous cars would soon rule the road, companies whose tech underpinned them have shifted to other industries, like managing shipyards and city traffic.
item.dc:creator: John R. Quain
item.media:credit: Ilvy Njiokiktjien for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Port of Rotterdam in the Netherlands, where ground-penetrating radar is being tested for automation systems.
